title: tephroseris atropurpurea subsp. tomentosa
intro: Tephroseris atropurpurea subsp. tomentosa is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and as a mild laxative, due to its saponin and glycoside content. The health benefits of Tephroseris atropurpurea subsp. tomentosa include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be dried and used to make teas or infusions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
